kicker: PT LEVEL II // DISADVANTAGES OF THE METHODS

beats.narration: Level One ran the process. Level Two chooses the method — and every choice is a bet, because each one has a built-in way to miss flaws.

payload.title: EVERY METHOD FAILS DIFFERENTLY
payload.sub: Pick the failure mode you can live with.

beats.narration: Method A's signature failure is overwash. Emulsifier is blended in, so flaw-trapped dye stays water-removable — and seconds of extra spray strip a broad, shallow flaw clean.

payload.heading: Overwash empties the shallow flaw
payload.note: PENETRANT STAYS WASHABLE → SHALLOW FLAW STRIPS → PART LOOKS CLEAN

beats.narration: Each removal method has its price. A can't be controlled locally. B and D buy control with critical timing and refractometer checks. C is slow, by hand.
Type bills you too. Fluorescent pays a lighting tax: dark booth, verified UV, dark adaptation. Visible dye sacrifices sensitivity, and must never precede a fluorescent exam.

chips.s: No graded levels; tight cracks hide

beats.narration: So read the part before you pick. Match each method's weakness against the flaw, the surface, and the place — then let the procedure rule.

payload.sub: Match the weakness to the job — the procedure rules.
